soliton.text: Regex character class `_[a-zA-Z-_]*\.properties` uses `-` in a way that creates an unintended ASCII range (`Z-_`), potentially mis-matching locale suffixes and resolving the wrong English source file
Locale-suffix stripping regex uses `*` instead of `+`, allowing an empty suffix so a filename like `_.properties` would incorrectly match

Path construction uses `file.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("resources-community", "resources")`, which can replace unintended occurrences outside the intended directory segment and resolve the wrong `englishFile`
`verifySafeHtml()` catches `IOException` and rethrows `RuntimeException`, inconsistent with the mojo’s `MojoExecutionException` error handling and leading to unhelpful Maven failure output

`POLICY_SOME_HTML`, `POLICY_NO_HTML`, and `HTML_TAGS` are named like constants but are non-static, non-final, and not private, causing unnecessary per-instance rebuilding and inconsistent style with other constants
Method name typo `santizeAnchors` should be `sanitizeAnchors` (both declaration and call site)

AccessTokenContext constructor mistakenly calls Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed") instead of validating rawTokenId, allowing null rawTokenId to propagate and corrupt token IDs
AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId test matcher uses wrong substring indices (substring(3,5) instead of (4,6)) so it reads the wrong grant shortcut portion of the prefix
AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId test matcher has inverted boolean logic (returns false when the shortcut matches), causing grant-type assertions to be silently voided and tests to pass/fail incorrectly

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider dereferences userSession without a null-check even though getUserSession() can return null for detached/transient sessions, leading to NPEs and HTTP 500s during token issuance

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory collision detection for grant shortcuts is broken because ConcurrentHashMap.put overwrites existing entries before size-based collision checks, allowing duplicate shortcuts to slip through and later fail decoding

OAuth2GrantTypeFactory SPI adds getShortcut() as an abstract method without a default implementation, causing binary incompatibility and AbstractMethodError at startup for third-party/out-of-tree implementations
OAuth2GrantType.Context copy constructor was removed from the public SPI, causing NoSuchMethodError/runtime linkage failures (and compile breaks) for external extensions that clone/wrap Context

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider token-id decoding treats legacy tokens gracefully but throws IllegalArgumentException for unknown encoded shortcuts, breaking rolling upgrades/heterogeneous clusters by turning unknown prefixes into HTTP 500s

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider.getTokenContextFromTokenId throws on attacker-controlled token IDs and includes the full raw token in exception messages, risking log injection/data exposure and unhandled 500s

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory lazy shortcut-map refresh updates two ConcurrentHashMaps without synchronization, allowing races that break the bijection invariant between grantsByShortcuts and grantsToShortcuts and can miss collisions
AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper changed getShouldUseLightweightToken from an overridable instance method to a public static method, silently disconnecting subclass overrides due to static method hiding

CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential serializes and sends raw plaintext recovery codes to user-storage providers via updateCredential, causing cleartext persistence/exposure of long-lived bearer secrets
RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean calls Optional.get() without checking presence when no recovery credential exists, risking NoSuchElementException and a 500 during login form rendering

CredentialHelper stores a JSON array/List<String> of raw codes as credentialData, but RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.createFromCredentialModel expects hashed RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialData JSON, causing deserialization/type mismatch and potential NPE/auth failures
BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage.isValid validates a recovery code but never invalidates/removes the used code, allowing infinite replay of one-time recovery codes

CredentialHelper hard-codes the provider ID string "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes" instead of using RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialProviderFactory.PROVIDER_ID, risking drift and null provider/NPE if the ID changes

CredentialHelper uses updateCredential’s boolean return to infer whether federated storage handled persistence, conflating 'not handled' with 'handled but failed' and potentially causing double-writes or skipping local credential creation

RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential prefers federated credentials over local without warning, so after adding/migrating to a federated provider a stale/absent federated credential can shadow a valid local credential and lock users out

CLI exit-code contract is silently broken by changing RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE from 4 to 3 and reusing 4 for FEATURE_DISABLED, which can cause existing automation branching on exit code 4 to misbehave
No deprecation/upgrade-guide documentation is provided for the external breaking change in update-compatibility exit codes between minor releases

testFeatureNotEnabled only asserts the error message and does not assert the CLI exit code, leaving the new FEATURE_DISABLED numeric contract (and the recreate-path exit code) untested and prone to regression

operator/scripts/Dockerfile-custom-image bakes --features=rolling-updates into kc.sh build for all derived images, potentially enabling a preview feature by default and contradicting the intended gating if the Dockerfile is user-facing/undocumented

ClientRemovedEvent cleanup calls ClientPermissionsV2.setPermissionsEnabled(..., false) which unconditionally throws UnsupportedOperationException in V2, potentially aborting client deletion transactions

Wrapping the entire cleanup listener in Profile.Feature.ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ disables RoleRemoved/ClientRemoved/GroupRemoved cleanup for deployments using other fine-grained-authz flag variants, causing orphan Resource/Policy records to accumulate

ClientPermissionsV2.hasPermission(ClientModel, String) can throw NullPointerException when the per-type wildcard "Clients" resource is missing because getResourceTypeResource(...) may return null and the result is passed into policyStore.findByResource without a null guard

ClientPermissionsV2.getClientsWithPermission omits clients that are authorized only via the wildcard "Clients" resource because findByType returns only per-client resources, leading to under-reported authorized client sets

ClientPermissionsV2.canMapRoles/canMapCompositeRoles/canMapClientScopeRoles lack the MANAGE_CLIENTS admin-role fast-path present in V1, causing admins with only MANAGE_CLIENTS to be denied role-mapping unless explicit MAP_ROLES* policies exist

ClientPermissionsV2 routes ClientScopeModel view/manage checks through the Clients resource type instead of a dedicated ClientScopes resource type, widening access so principals with client-wide permissions implicitly gain visibility/manage over all client scopes
GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() incorrectly treats having only VIEW scope on the all-groups resource as sufficient for MANAGE, enabling read-to-write privilege escalation
getGroupIdsWithViewPermission adds authorization Resource UUIDs (resource.getId()) instead of group model IDs (resource.getName()/group.getId()), breaking downstream logic that expects group IDs

Because getGroupIdsWithViewPermission returns UUIDs, hasPermission(groupResource.getId(), ...) looks up by name using a UUID, misses the specific-group resource, and silently falls back to type-level (all-groups) permission checks
UsersResource.searchForUser removed the per-user usersEvaluator.canView post-filter, allowing QUERY_USERS-only callers without view/manage roles or group-scoped view permission to enumerate all users
BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser has the same missing canView post-filter pattern, creating the same user-enumeration authorization bypass risk

UserPermissionsV2 canView/canManage no longer short-circuit for AdminRoles.ADMIN, potentially causing admins with only ADMIN (non-standard composites) to get unexpected 403s unless fine-grained permissions are configured

AdminPermissions.registerListener now registers the listener only if the feature flag is enabled at registration time, so enabling the feature later (tests/profile reload) would skip cleanup events and leave orphaned permission resources

ModelToRepresentation.searchGroupModelsByAttributes was removed as a public static helper, creating a binary-incompatible change for downstream extensions; should be kept as a deprecated delegate or documented in migration notes

Adding abstract method order() to public CryptoProvider SPI is source/binary incompatible and can break third-party implementations (compile failures or AbstractMethodError); should be a default method for backward compatibility
AuthzClientCryptoProvider.getEcdsaCryptoProvider().concatenatedRSToASN1DER contains dead/orphan ASN1Encoder allocations whose results are discarded, misleading readers and wasting work

CryptoIntegration.detectProvider behavior changed from fail-fast on multiple providers to silently selecting by highest order(), which can hide misconfiguration and lead to non-deterministic provider choice when orders tie; should throw on ties and/or log ignored providers at warn

ASN1Decoder.readLength() returns -1 for indefinite-length (0x80) and callers pass it to read(length), causing NegativeArraySizeException instead of a clear IOException; DER forbids indefinite length so it should fail fast with IOException

ECDSAAlgorithmTest lacks negative/malformed-input tests for the new hand-rolled ASN.1 parser (e.g., invalid tags, high-tag-form, indefinite/oversized lengths, wrong element count, length mismatches, truncated inputs), leaving security-sensitive failure modes untested
GroupAdapter.getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when modelSupplier.get() returns null, which can trigger NullPointerException in callers that auto-unbox to primitive long or call longValue()
GroupTest.java reader thread lacks a timeout/join, risking hanging/flaky tests

UsernameForm.authenticate() calls isConditionalPasskeysEnabled() with zero arguments, but only a one-argument overload (isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)) is defined in the PR, which may cause a compile error if no zero-arg overload exists in the superclass chain

UsernameForm.authenticate() likely should pass context.getUser() into isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(...) to match intended behavior (suppress fast-path skip only when passkeys are enabled for the current user)
Unused helper method `_create_process_for_shard(self, shard: int)` is defined but never called, risking drift and dead code
Metric tag key is inconsistent for the same shard dimension (`shard` vs `shards`) across emitted metrics, fragmenting dashboards/alerts
Metric tag value shape is inconsistent for the shard dimension (single-shard integer vs comma-joined multi-shard string), causing inconsistent aggregation/grouping
`SpanFlusher.__init__` accepts a caller-supplied `SpansBuffer` but effectively discards it (only reads `assigned_shards`) and constructs new per-process buffers instead

Creating new per-process buffers and pickling them into child processes multiplies Redis client/state (up to ~3 copies per shard group) and silently drops any initialization/state on the caller-provided buffer

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result can slice a Django QuerySet with a negative start offset on backward pagination, triggering Django's guaranteed AssertionError for negative indexing and causing a 500/DoS via attacker-controlled cursor.offset
Inline comments in paginator.py incorrectly claim Django ORM handles negative slicing/boundary conditions automatically, contradicting Django docs and masking the crash risk
OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key applies math.floor/math.ceil to a datetime field (e.g., order_by='-datetime'), raising TypeError and crashing audit log pagination during cursor construction
organization_auditlogs.py dereferences organization_context.member.has_global_access without checking member for None, causing AttributeError for non-superusers without active org membership
Authorization gate incorrectly uses member.has_global_access as a privilege check, allowing ordinary members (e.g., open-membership orgs) to access the advanced/optimized audit log pagination path

BasePaginator.get_result clamps negative forward cursor offsets to 0, silently changing behavior from loud failure to wrong results and potentially hiding cursor-corruption bugs (should at least warn/log)

Comments in cursors.py/paginator.py falsely claim negative offsets enable efficient reverse/bidirectional pagination; negative offsets crash and offset-based reverse pagination is not efficient for large datasets
OptimizedCursorPaginator docstring falsely advertises streamlined boundary handling and an optimized query path despite being a near-copy with crashing branches and misleading maintainers

Lock narrowing in bleveBackend.BuildIndex introduces a TOCTOU race where concurrent calls for the same key can both build an index and the second overwrites/discards the first, causing wasted work and incorrect behavior
Concurrent same-key BuildIndex can double-increment metrics (IndexMetrics.IndexTenants) because both goroutines run the full build path

For file-backed indexes (bleve.New with dir based on Root/Namespace/Resource.Group), concurrent same-key builds can target the same directory, leading to failures, duplicate handles, orphaned/corrupted on-disk state

NewResourceServer now runs s.Init(ctx) synchronously using the caller-provided context, so if that context has a deadline/cancellation (request/test scoped), index initialization can be aborted and server construction fails

Removing per-method Init guards means callers that ignore NewResourceServer’s error return or construct the server directly (e.g., in tests) lose the previous lazy-init safety net and may use an uninitialized server

MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but implements none of its abstract methods (get_dedupe_value, get_group_key_values, build_occurrence_and_event_data), so instantiating it raises a TypeError and breaks metric-alert detector evaluation

process_detectors assumes handler.evaluate() returns a dict and calls .values(); any DetectorHandler subclass still returning a list will crash at runtime with AttributeError ('list' object has no attribute values')
Dataclass field `queued` uses `timezone.now()` as a bare default, so it’s evaluated once at import time and shared across instances instead of being set per instance
`to_dict()` returns a `datetime` in the dict, which is not JSON-serializable for Celery’s default JSON serializer and can raise a `TypeError` when enqueueing the task

`from_dict()` can accept a serialized `queued` value as a string and construct an `AssignmentSource` where `queued` is a `str` (types not enforced), silently violating the expected `datetime` type/contract

Calling `installation.should_sync("outbound_assignee", parsed_assignment_source)` with a new positional argument can break integration subclasses that still implement the old 1-parameter `should_sync(self, attribute)` signature, causing a `TypeError` at runtime
Uses Python 3.13-only APIs (`queue.ShutDown` exception and `Queue.shutdown()`), causing `AttributeError` on Python <=3.12 and leaving worker threads blocked on `queue.get()` during shutdown
Shutdown logic joins daemon worker threads with a timeout while they may still be processing, so the process can tear down workers mid-task and drop in-flight work

`FixedQueuePool.shutdown()` closes/stops workers without draining already-submitted items; `wait_until_empty()` exists but is not called from close/terminate paths, leading to abandoned outstanding offsets and stalled commits

Because offsets are added to `OffsetTracker` before enqueueing, abandoned items remain outstanding and the commit loop cannot advance; on restart Kafka re-reads them, causing duplicate processing after deploys

`OffsetTracker` never evicts per-partition state on Kafka partition revocation/rebalance, so `all_offsets`, `outstanding`, `last_committed`, and `partition_locks` grow unbounded and leak memory/locks over time

`FixedQueuePool.submit()` records an offset via `add_offset()` before the work item is safely enqueued; if `put()` or grouping/hash logic raises, offsets can become committable even though the work was never executed (silent loss)
`_commit_loop` has a shutdown race: when `shutdown_event` is set it can exit without performing a final `get_committable_offsets()`/commit pass, dropping the last chunk of commit progress
OptimizedCursorPaginator uses a negative start_offset for queryset slicing when cursor.offset < 0, triggering Django QuerySet AssertionError and crashing paginated requests
BasePaginator allows negative offset to reach queryset slicing when cursor.is_prev is true, enabling a trivially reachable DoS across paginated endpoints via AssertionError
Lua add-buffer script uses ZUNIONSTORE without AGGREGATE MAX (defaults to SUM), inflating scores for spans present in both sets and corrupting eviction ordering

Cursor.__init__ no longer validates non-negative offsets from attacker-controlled cursor strings, allowing crafted negative offsets to propagate through pagination and potentially cause crashes or unintended negative-index behavior on non-QuerySet sequences

Audit-log endpoint’s authorization gate for optimized/advanced pagination is too permissive (allows has_global_access members), enabling non-staff users to trigger the advanced paginator path and associated risks

_load_segment_data removed the max_segment_spans guard, eliminating defense-in-depth/telemetry and allowing oversized segments to be loaded without bounds if Lua-side eviction is bypassed or legacy data exists
Git submodule `sentry-repo` is added as a gitlink without a corresponding `.gitmodules` entry, breaking `git submodule update --init` and CI checkouts
`_is_error_focused_query` uses a naive substring check (`"event.type:error" in query.lower()`), misclassifying negated/compound/quoted queries and incorrectly rewriting to `upsampled_count()`
60-second allowlist eligibility cache can keep upsampling enabled after a project is removed from the allowlist; `invalidate_upsampling_cache` exists but is not wired to option changes
Cache key uses Python `hash(tuple(...))`, which is process-local/unstable for non-int tuple contents and can cause inconsistent keys across workers; should use stable serialization
`_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling` uses `except Exception: pass`, silently swallowing unexpected errors and causing tests to skip setting `sample_rate` without noticing
An unrelated user-facing typo/message fix is mixed into the feature PR, increasing diff noise and complicating reverts; should be a separate PR
`transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling` is redundantly called in multiple branches; should compute once to avoid duplication and missed branches

`upsampled_count` assumes `sample_weight` is non-null for all rows; NULL `sample_weight` rows will be skipped by `sum`, causing silent undercounting unless handled (e.g., `ifNull`/`sumIf`) or enforced end-to-end

Denial-cache key construction in userPermDenialCacheKey uses '_' as a delimiter even though components may contain '_', causing cache-key collisions between distinct (action,name,parent) tuples and incorrect denied results
Permission denial cache entries are never invalidated/purged when permissions are later granted, so users can remain denied until shortCacheTTL expires (stale 403 after grant)
permissionCacheUsage("false") metric is now emitted for cache-hit-but-denied cases as well as true cache misses, silently changing metric semantics and inflating the 'false' counter

Check treats any error from getCachedIdentityPermissions as a cache miss, swallowing real lookup/identifier errors (e.g., GetUserIdentifiers failures or unsupported identity type), causing redundant failing calls and misattributed metrics/logging
Uncaught HTTPError (e.g., 401/403/5xx/timeout) from get_user_info() propagates and crashes the OAuth install flow with a 500 instead of rendering a graceful error page
Direct key access integration.metadata["sender"]["login"] can raise KeyError when metadata is missing/different shape, causing a 500 (and potential info disclosure) instead of failing closed
Race condition: OAuth callback may arrive before the installation webhook persists integration.metadata["sender"]["login"], causing legitimate installs to fail (invalid request or KeyError)
Deletion of FORWARD_INSTALL_FOR constant in pipeline_advancer.py can break external/internal importers via ImportError
OAuth state uses pipeline.signature (not a fresh single-use nonce), enabling replay within the pipeline window and weakening CSRF protection
OAuth state comparison uses plain != instead of constant-time comparison, creating a potential timing side-channel
Test test_github_user_mismatch mutates webhook payload but uses a hardcoded HMAC signature that won’t match the mutated body, so webhook validation will fail and the test will break
installation_id can be overwritten via request.GET on the OAuth callback leg, allowing a mid-flow installation swap and undermining identity binding
Bare except Exception during token exchange parsing silently swallows real failures (network/parse/programming errors) without logging, hindering debugging and incident response
Test cleanup callback uses literal "alias" instead of the loop alias variable, so created IDPs are never removed and leak across test runs, causing flaky count-based assertions

Cache-hit path in getForLogin() returns raw IdentityProviderModel objects while cache-miss/fallback returns OrganizationAwareIdentityProviderModel-wrapped objects, causing inconsistent behavior between cache hits and misses

Login cache is not invalidated when an IDP’s organization membership changes via OrganizationProvider add/removeIdentityProvider (without going through IdentityProviderStorageProvider.update()), leaving stale cached login IDP sets
getLoginPredicate() dereferences idp.getConfig() without a null check, risking an NPE if getConfig() can be null on create/update/remove invalidation paths

getLoginPredicate() must remain aligned with JpaIdentityProviderStorageProvider.getForLogin() DB filtering; misalignment could allow IDPs returned by JPA that fail the predicate, preventing proper cache invalidation and leaving stale entries
Test scenario 4 conflates update() and addIdentityProvider(), so it cannot verify that addIdentityProvider() alone triggers cache invalidation
Tests do not cover cache invalidation when removing a login-eligible IDP (they only remove a disabled/non-eligible IDP), so regressions in remove-path invalidation could go undetected
enableSqlExpressions always returns false, so the feature-flag lookup has no effect and SQL expressions are permanently disabled
enableSqlExpressions assigns enabled as the negation of the flag state, creating inverted/misleading control flow that could cause accidental re-enablement if simplified later
No comment documents that SQL expressions are intentionally disabled as a CVE-2024-9264 mitigation, making the security intent easy to miss
No regression test pins the contract that SQL expressions must remain disabled even if FlagSqlExpressions is set, so CI won’t catch accidental re-enablement

pkg/expr/sql/parser.go retains a vulnerable fmt.Sprintf-based SQL string interpolation pattern with naive quote escaping, which would reintroduce CVE-2024-9264 if the stub is ever replaced with a real engine
TablesList still builds/exposes the dormant vulnerable SQL command shape instead of early-returning an explicit “disabled” error
Error messages like "sqlExpressions is not implemented" / "not implemented" are misleading because the feature is intentionally disabled for CVE mitigation and the messages lack advisory context
ReadQuery rejects SQL expressions without an explicit warning/log signal, causing existing alert rules to fail silently and making operator debugging harder
Missing observability (e.g., a counter metric) for rejected SQL expressions, limiting operators’ ability to detect and track attempted use/migration needs

Double-checked locking is incomplete: after acquiring the write lock, the code does not re-check whether another goroutine has already populated entryPointAssetsCache, causing redundant reloads during cold-start bursts
On error, the code unconditionally assigns entryPointAssetsCache = result even when result is nil, which can overwrite and clear a previously valid cache entry and force repeated reloads thereafter
Cleanup ticker interval was reduced from 10 minutes to 1 minute in pkg/services/cleanup/cleanup.go:77, likely an unintended debug leftover and increases risk of DB lock contention/deadlocks
Six routine cleanup-progress log messages are emitted at Error level on the happy path in pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:239, polluting error logs and triggering false alerts
A log statement on the happy path includes an "err" field where err is guaranteed to be nil, creating misleading logs
Happy-path logging includes full ID slices ("ids", ids), which can cause log flooding and increased ingestion costs on large tables

SQLite >999-parameter branch builds the IN(...) list via repeated fmt.Sprintf string concatenation in a loop (O(n^2)), causing unnecessary CPU/memory overhead in pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:323
Cleanup interval is hardcoded (no config/feature flag/comment), removing an operator tuning knob; if a faster cadence is desired it should be configurable and documented

Async legacy write goroutines derive their context from the cancellable request context, so the context is canceled when the handler returns and legacy Create/Update/Delete/DeleteCollection calls are silently dropped
Create error path records a legacy-duration/error metric even though the failure occurred in storage (should record storage duration)
Update error path records a legacy-duration/error metric even though the failure occurred in storage (should record storage duration)
Delete passes the object name as the Prometheus 'kind' label, creating unbounded/high-cardinality metrics and risking OOM/scrape failures
DeleteCollection async legacy goroutine records storage duration instead of legacy duration, double-counting storage and leaving legacy histogram empty
Update no longer wraps objInfo with an updateWrapper carrying the storage-persisted object, so Legacy recomputes the updated object from its own pre-image and can diverge from Storage
Delete attaches the base logger (d.Log) to the context instead of the enriched per-call logger, so downstream klog.FromContext(ctx) misses name/kind/method fields
Async goroutines lack panic recovery; a panic inside the goroutine can crash the entire apiserver process
Tests reference a bare 'p' prometheus registry whose declaration may be missing after Mode 1 changes, potentially breaking compilation
Sharing a package-level/mutable Prometheus registry across tests can cause duplicate collector registration panics, especially with parallel subtests

Federated rule detection was removed in useAllRulerRuleAbilities (isFederated hardcoded to false), causing federated Mimir rule groups to be treated as mutable and enabling Edit/Delete/Pause actions that will fail against the backend
React key prop is missing for GrafanaRuleListItem in FilterView’s list rendering, leading to React ‘unique key’ warnings and unstable row identity/state across re-renders
Grafana rule detail links no longer include the returnTo query parameter, so navigating to a rule detail loses the filtered-list context and the Back flow won’t return users to the same list state
TOCTOU race in CreateOrUpdateDevice: CountDevices check and subsequent INSERT/UPSERT are separate statements without transaction/locking, allowing concurrent requests to exceed device_limit

Authenticate now calls TagDevice synchronously on the request context, so slow/stalled DB operations can block anonymous authentication indefinitely (or until DB timeout), increasing latency and enabling DoS amplification
Using the request context for TagDevice means client disconnect cancels ctx mid INSERT/UPDATE, potentially leaving inconsistent writes and generating cancellation errors
Removal of panic-recovery around TagDevice means a panic in the tagging path can crash the auth handler

updateDevice returns ErrDeviceLimitReached when rowsAffected==0, conflating device-not-found or stale-window misses with actual device-limit enforcement, breaking error semantics/logging and auth-layer contract
anonymousDeviceExpiration constant is duplicated in anonstore and api packages, creating two sources of truth that can drift and cause inconsistent expiration/listing behavior
When device_limit>0, CreateOrUpdateDevice performs an extra CountDevices COUNT(*) query on every anonymous request, adding per-request DB load on a high-RPS path

Frontend anonymousDeviceLimit is typed as number|undefined while backend always sends a numeric field (0 when disabled), so feature gating based on undefined will be incorrect; TS type/default should match backend or backend should use omitempty/pointer

I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! will raise NoMethodError at runtime if any environment file still sets config.i18n.fallbacks = true, because Rails after_initialize overwrites I18n.fallbacks with I18n::Locale::Fallbacks (which lacks ensure_loaded!)
Background jobs/rake tasks set I18n.locale without calling I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!, so fallback locales are never loaded outside web requests and missing keys won’t fall back to site locale/:en

FallbackLocaleList (Hash subclass) does not implement I18n::Locale::Fallbacks#defaults, so I18n::Backend::Fallbacks calling I18n.fallbacks.defaults may raise NoMethodError during translation fallback resolution

FallbackLocaleList#[] calls SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym unconditionally; if default_locale is nil during boot/tests/unavailable settings, it raises NoMethodError (NilClass#to_sym) and can crash requests early in initialization

`traceID` is no longer included in the "Plugin Request Completed" log line because the new logging path (`m.logger.FromContext(ctx).Info(...)` + `instrumentContext`) does not attach `traceID`, risking loss of trace correlation for plugin request logs

`grafanaInfraLogWrapper.FromContext` silently falls back to `d.New()` when the underlying logger type assertion fails, returning a fresh logger with no contextual attributes and no warning/metric, which can silently drop `pluginId`, `endpoint`, `dsUID`, `dsName`, and `uname` from logs

`TestLogger.FromContext` in `pkg/plugins/log/fake.go` returns a new `NewTestLogger()` instance instead of returning itself, so tests that assert against `f.InfoLogs`/`f.ErrorLogs` can miss log lines emitted via the `FromContext` path

No tests were added for `ContextualLoggerMiddleware`, so nothing verifies that each endpoint wrapper calls `instrumentContext` (and that streaming methods intentionally skip it), allowing regressions where contextual attributes are not attached to the request context
Test/file naming is inconsistent after the rename: the test file was renamed to `metrics_middleware_test.go` but the top-level test function is still named `TestInstrumentationMiddleware`
SQL injection risk in migration due to interpolating admin-writable site_settings.value directly into raw INSERT statements
Migration can break or be exploited when a host value contains a single quote because the raw SQL string is not escaped/parameterized
Controller fetches EmbeddableHost with where(...).first and then calls destroy/save_host on nil, causing NoMethodError and 500 instead of 404 on invalid id
Potential information leak via 500 + stack trace when nil dereference occurs in controller actions (destroy/update)
Controller reads params[:embeddable_host][:host] directly without strong parameters (require/permit) and without guarding missing parent key, risking errors and unsafe mass assignment patterns
Authorization may be insufficient: ensure_staff may be too weak for managing embed allowlist; admin-only access should be considered
Model before_validation callback calls host.sub! unconditionally; if host is nil it raises NoMethodError during validation instead of producing a validation error
Frontend store hydration calls self.pluralize(...) but pluralize is undefined on the store object, causing runtime TypeError and breaking all _ids hydration
Frontend store hydration calls obj[k].map(...) without guarding for null/undefined arrays, causing TypeError when server returns null for an _ids field
Migration uses create_table ... force: true, which can drop the table and silently delete all embeddable_host records when migration is re-run

Fabricator definitions are in the wrong files (category_fabricator.rb contains embeddable_host and embeddable_host_fabricator.rb contains category fabricators), creating a maintainability trap and potential duplicate-fabricator load errors
record_for_host does not normalize hostname case before querying lower(host), causing allowlist mismatches/bypass of legitimate entries when referer host contains uppercase letters
Host validation regex incorrectly limits TLD length to 5 characters, rejecting valid modern TLDs
Host validation regex permits path suffixes (/(\/.*)?/), allowing non-host content to be stored and potentially reflected unsafely (XSS risk)
Host validation regex does not reject @ userinfo components, allowing potentially unsafe/ambiguous host strings

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291 for `.reply-count a`, the light-theme value was incorrectly changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%)`, unintentionally lightening the link color in light mode

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291 for `.reply-count a`, the dark-theme arm uses `scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%)` but should be the inverted value `70%` (100-30) to match the intended transformation

In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/modal.scss:102 for `.custom-message-length`, the `dark-light-choose` arguments are inverted, causing the light-theme color to become `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` instead of preserving the original `70%`

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/user.scss:523 for `.name`, the light-theme value was incorrectly changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%)`, removing the intended visual distinction from `.title`

In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/topic-post.scss:183 for `h3`, the light-theme value was incorrectly changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 20%)` to `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%)`, significantly lightening headings in light mode

In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/user.scss:498 for `.name`, the light-theme value was incorrectly changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%)`, making it visually identical to `.title` in light mode
GroupsController#members uses an unbounded `limit` query param, enabling a DoS by requesting extremely large page sizes

Admin GroupsController `add_members`/`remove_member` mutate membership rows before `group.save`, so a failed save can return an error while the DB membership changes have already been applied (non-transactional write)
Admin UI pagination computes `totalPages` as `Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1`, which is off-by-one when `user_count` is an exact multiple of `limit` and can show an extra empty page
Group model `findMembers` returns `undefined` when group name is empty instead of a resolved promise/array, breaking callers that chain `.then(...)`
Group create/update endpoints changed parameter shape from nested `params[:group]` to top-level params without versioning/deprecation, risking breaking external API consumers
GroupsController#members response shape changed from a bare array to `{members: [...], meta: {...}}` without versioning/deprecation, risking breaking external API consumers
Admin route `/admin/groups/:id/users` was removed; any remaining callers (plugins/themes/external) will 404 unless the route is kept or deprecated

Missing tests for new/changed behaviors: updating non-automatic groups via new top-level params, add_members with nonexistent usernames, remove_member when user isn’t a member, and members pagination metadata across pages
Minor JS style/correctness nit: `if (!this.get('id')) { return };` has a stray semicolon/brace relying on ASI; should be `return;`
Non-atomic read-modify-write of BlockedEmail.match_count in should_block? can lose increments under concurrent requests (race condition)
should_block? ignores the return value of record.save, potentially swallowing persistence failures
should_block? is called from EmailValidator#validate_each and can run multiple times per registration attempt (multiple valid?/save calls), inflating match_count even without concurrency
Email validation builds a regex from SiteSetting while escaping only '.', allowing regex metacharacters through and enabling ReDoS via catastrophic backtracking
Misconfigured SiteSetting patterns can produce an invalid regex and raise RegexpError on signup, causing a denial of service until fixed

create_account_controller.js pushes result.values.email without checking it exists, potentially adding undefined to rejectedEmails and causing spurious invalid state when accountEmail is undefined/empty
User profile template binds to `model.website_name` but Discourse JS models camelize serializer fields to `websiteName`, so the website name will render as empty/undefined
Ruby serializer uses mutating concatenation (`"." << website_host`) on a string literal, which can raise `FrozenError` under `# frozen_string_literal: true` and is semantically misleading
`URI(website.to_s)` is parsed multiple times per call and only the first `.host` access is rescue-guarded; later `.path` parsing/access can raise unrescued exceptions and is inefficient

Docstring annotation in `user.js` was changed from `@property websiteName` to `@property profileBackground`, potentially removing/misleading documentation for an existing `websiteName` computed property
Use of Ruby Kernel#open (open-uri) on untrusted URLs enables SSRF and potential command execution (e.g., leading '|' or internal IPs) in feed polling and remote embed import paths
RSS/remote embed content is stored and rendered as raw HTML via a new raw_html cook_method that bypasses sanitization, enabling stored XSS
embed.html.erb uses request.referer as postMessage targetOrigin (invalid because it’s a full URL, not an origin), causing the handshake to fail
request.referer is interpolated into a single-quoted JavaScript string without escaping in embed.html.erb, allowing JS injection via a crafted Referer header
Parent-side postMessage origin validation uses indexOf substring matching instead of strict origin equality in embed.js, allowing attacker origins to bypass the check
Embed access control relies only on Referer host comparison in ensure_embeddable, which is suppressible/spoofable and not a reliable authorization mechanism
Controller sets X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL, which is not a valid directive and won’t reliably control framing; should use CSP frame-ancestors instead
poll_feed job calls i.content.scrub without handling missing <content> elements, causing NoMethodError on feeds that only provide <description>
Redis throttling uses SETNX followed by a separate EXPIRE, risking a permanent key with no TTL if a crash/failover occurs between commands
Migration uses create_table ... force: true, which can drop existing data when migrations are replayed/reset
Concurrent imports of the same embed_url can race and raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique, causing a 500 and potentially leaving an orphan topic
ERB template contains '<%- end if %>' which is a Ruby syntax error and prevents the template from rendering

Duplicate OptimizedImage.self.downsize method definitions cause the second definition to silently override the original 4-argument signature, breaking existing callers and potentially crashing when opts is treated as an Integer

UploadsController remote URL download cap is hardcoded to 10MB, bypassing admin-configured SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb and allowing larger-than-configured downloads to be fetched and written to disk

Client-side upload size checks are hardcoded to 10MB, ignoring Discourse.SiteSettings max_<type>_size_kb/max_image_size_kb, making the type-specific limit ineffective and causing incorrect 413 messaging

OptimizedImage.downsize accepts an unvalidated dimensions string that flows into shell execution (ImageMagick convert command), widening shell-injection/RCE risk if any future caller passes untrusted input
Downsize retry loop in UploadsController lacks a convergence/no-progress guard and does not check convert success, so it may burn all attempts and still proceed with an oversized file
Downsize is performed in-place using the same tempfile.path for source and destination, risking file corruption/0-byte output and ambiguous/stale size checks

Invoking ImageMagick convert up to 5 times per upload increases resource-exhaustion and ImageTragick attack surface without sufficient pre-validation (MIME/pixel dimensions) or hardened ImageMagick policy limits
Unsubscribe action uses TopicUser.find_by which can return nil, causing NoMethodError when accessing tu.notification_level and calling tu.save! for users without an existing TopicUser row
Unsubscribe endpoint is a GET route that mutates server state, making it vulnerable to CSRF and unintended triggering by link previewers/URL scanners
Email locale string requires %{unsubscribe_url} interpolation, which will raise I18n::MissingInterpolationArgument for mailers that enable add_unsubscribe_link but do not pass unsubscribe_url
dropdown-button component concatenates unescaped title into HTML, enabling stored XSS via user-controlled topic titles
unsubscribe.hbs uses triple-brace {{{stopNotificiationsText}}} to render an I18n string that interpolates an unescaped topic title, enabling XSS
Adding render :show inside shared perform_show_response forces any caller to render the show template, creating confusing coupling and breaking future reuse

Unsubscribe action does not check topic authorization (guardian.ensure_can_see!) and lacks a nil-guard for missing topics, enabling private-topic existence probing and undefined behavior for invalid topic_id
SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId is called with empty data {}, making updateMany a no-op so Prisma @updatedAt does not bump and the cache-last-updated indicator never refreshes
scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh uses BSD/macOS sed in-place syntax (sed -i '' -E), which fails on GNU sed/Linux and prevents rewriting the .env file

connectedCalendars.handler.ts directly instantiates CalendarCacheRepository, bypassing the feature-flagged CalendarCacheRepositoryMock and causing DB access (and possible runtime errors) even when the calendar-cache feature is disabled

deleteCache.handler.ts authorization only checks credential ownership via userId and does not account for team-shared or delegation credentials, enabling IDOR/broken access control where users can delete cache for shared/delegation credentials
connectedCalendars.handler.ts uses `|| null` on cacheStatusMap.get(...), which can incorrectly coerce falsy values; should use nullish coalescing (`??`) for correctness/future-proofing
Working-hours boundary check computes both start and end from slotStartTime, never using slotEndTime, so slots that extend past workingHour.endTime can be incorrectly marked available
Day-off override check compares two Dayjs instances with === (reference equality), so the start==end branch never triggers and blocked days can remain bookable
utcOffset is multiplied by -1, inverting the timezone shift direction and causing date-override matching to use the wrong local day/time
DST boundary bug: utcOffset sampled only at override.start is reused for override.end, causing override end times to be off by ±60 minutes across DST transitions
deleteMany cleanup predicate uses `retryCount > 1` without filtering `method: SMS`, so the SMS cron can delete EMAIL/WHATSAPP reminders from the shared `WorkflowReminder` table

If Twilio send succeeds but the subsequent Prisma update fails, the catch path increments `retryCount` without marking the reminder scheduled or saving the SID, causing the SMS to be resent (duplicate SMS) on the next cron tick

The `await prisma.workflowReminder.update(...)` inside the catch block is unguarded; if it throws, it propagates and aborts the remaining cron loop iterations and may prevent sending the HTTP response

`retryCount` is incremented via non-atomic read-modify-write (`reminder.retryCount + 1`) based on a stale value from `findMany`, so concurrent cron runs can lose increments and exceed intended retry limits
Retry deletion threshold `retryCount > 1` is an ambiguous/off-by-one magic number (unclear intended number of attempts); should use a clearer predicate/constant (e.g., `gte: MAX_SMS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS`)
`retryCount` column is global on `WorkflowReminder` but only SMS handler uses it; email/WhatsApp handlers don’t increment/clean up by it, creating divergent semantics and increasing risk of misuse
Exhausted reminders are silently deleted when retry limit is exceeded, with no logging/metrics/alerting for failed-to-deliver reminders
Logging the raw Twilio error object via `console.log` may leak sensitive data (e.g., recipient phone numbers/message bodies) into logs

Public InsightsBookingService.findMany() method was removed and replaced with getBaseConditions() without documenting/updating all external call sites, risking compile-time breaks and forcing callers to reimplement where-composition safely
Constructor public options type allows scope:"team" without teamId, but the internal zod discriminated union requires teamId for team scope, causing runtime ZodError despite compiling

Caching test suite (describe("Caching")) was deleted while caching fields/guards (cachedAuthConditions/cachedFilterConditions) remain in production code, reducing coverage and risking silent memoization regressions

Tests assert Prisma.Sql fragments via expect(...).toEqual(Prisma.sql`...`), which is fragile because it depends on Prisma.Sql internal structure that may change across Prisma versions; should compare normalized rendered SQL/params instead
Webhook handler trusts body-supplied userId, allowing any caller with the webhook secret to overwrite any user's stored OAuth credentials (IDOR/broken access control)
symmetricDecrypt is called with process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY || "", so an unset encryption key falls back to empty string and enables trivial/known-key decryption
Webhook secret check can be bypassed when CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET and the header are both unset because undefined !== undefined is false, allowing unauthenticated requests
Webhook secret is compared with a non-constant-time string comparison, enabling timing attacks to recover the secret
No rate limiting on the webhook endpoint, making timing attacks and request-flood DoS practical
No body-size cap on the webhook endpoint, increasing DoS risk and unnecessary processing
parseRefreshTokenResponse overwrites a missing refresh_token with the literal string "refresh_token", poisoning stored credentials when providers omit refresh_token on refresh

refreshOAuthTokens sync-path returns a raw fetch Response instead of parsed JSON and does not check response.ok/content-type, causing parseRefreshTokenResponse to fail/crash or potentially persist invalid data
Webhook endpoint lacks an HTTP method guard, so non-POST methods can reach parsing/decrypt/upsert logic and may leak secrets via logs/caches
symmetricDecrypt and JSON.parse are unguarded; malformed ciphertext/non-JSON causes 500s with distinguishable errors/statuses, creating an oracle and leaking details via response differences
refreshOAuthTokens fetches CALCOM_CREDENTIAL_SYNC_ENDPOINT without protocol/host validation, creating SSRF/misconfiguration risk and allowing attacker-controlled token JSON to be persisted
When credential sync is enabled but userId is null, refreshOAuthTokens silently falls back to direct refresh with no logging, hiding sync bypass/failures
minimumTokenResponseSchema only defines access_token but code mutates/relies on refresh_token, making the returned type inaccurate and potentially unsafe for callers

`deleteScheduledEmailReminder` with `immediateDelete=true` cancels the SendGrid scheduled send but returns without deleting/updating the corresponding `WorkflowReminder` DB row, leaving orphaned/stale reminders after reschedule

In `scheduleEmailReminders.ts`, a single failing `client.request` inside a loop-wide try/catch aborts the entire cancellation batch, skipping remaining reminders and skipping the trailing `Promise.all` cleanup, so one bad reminder can starve the cron batch

In `handleCancelBooking.ts`, reminder-cancellation promises are fired inside synchronous `.forEach` without `await`, so the handler can resolve before DB writes complete and rejections can become unhandled

The same fire-and-forget (dropped Promise) reminder-cancellation pattern exists in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx` and multiple call sites in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/workflows.tsx`, losing `Promise.all` synchronization

In `handleNewBooking.ts`, wrapping `forEach(async ...)` in `try/catch` cannot catch async rejections, making the error handling ineffective and allowing unhandled rejections (or dead-code catch if helpers swallow errors)
Permission check incorrectly computes isTeamAdminOrOwner using logical AND instead of OR, making the admin/owner authorization branch effectively unreachable
Guest email blacklist check is case-sensitive, allowing blacklisted addresses to bypass the blacklist via case variation
Attendee deduplication compares emails case-sensitively, allowing duplicate attendee rows when the same email is provided with different casing
Email sending uses the raw guests list instead of the filtered uniqueGuests list, causing emails to be sent to already-existing attendees and sending the wrong email template/type
AddGuests input schema has no maximum guest count (and no rate limiting), enabling abuse such as email-bombing and resource exhaustion
Email-send failures are logged with console.log without the error details and are swallowed, returning success even when notifications fail
Organizer email subject uses calEvent.attendees[0].name, which may be empty because newly added guests have name set to an empty string, producing a blank name in the subject

Authorization allows any attendee to add unlimited guests without organizer consent, enabling unwanted guest additions and leakage of event details (including video-call URL/password) to third parties
Backup codes are stored using reversible symmetric encryption instead of being hashed with a password-grade KDF, allowing plaintext recovery if the encryption key and DB are compromised
Backup-code consumption is not atomic (TOCTOU): concurrent login requests can reuse the same backup code before it is invalidated, enabling replay
Backup-code verification uses non-constant-time comparison via Array.indexOf, leaking timing information about match position/prefix
React state for backupCodes is initialized with useState([]) and inferred as never[], erasing type safety for string[] backup codes
resetState() does not clear sensitive React state (backupCodes, backupCodesUrl, dataUri, secret), leaving plaintext credentials in memory after modal close/cancel
Blob URL created for downloading backup codes is not revoked on close/unmount, causing memory leak and prolonged exposure of plaintext backup codes via an active object URL

EventManager.ts: unguarded access to mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration can throw when evt.destinationCalendar is empty or null, causing a runtime crash for Meet bookings without a personal destination calendar

GoogleCalendarService CalendarService.ts updateEvent: fallback logic uses a tautological find(cal.externalId === externalCalendarId) when externalCalendarId is falsy, so selectedCalendar becomes undefined and calendar.events.update is called with calendarId undefined

GoogleCalendarService CalendarService.ts deleteEvent: same tautological find fallback as updateEvent, so calendarId becomes undefined and calendar.events.delete may fail or target the wrong (primary) calendar; non-primary events may never be deleted

LarkCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing correct per-credential destinationCalendar selection for multi-host events

Office365CalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing correct per-credential destinationCalendar selection for multi-host events

BaseCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, breaking subclasses (e.g., CalDAV/Apple) that inherit it and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection

handleNewBooking.ts: initializing evt.destinationCalendar to null causes evt.destinationCalendar?.push(...teamDestinationCalendars) to no-op, silently dropping team hosts for COLLECTIVE events when organizer has no personal destination calendar

EventManager.ts updateAllCalendarEvents: after DB fallback, credential can remain undefined (e.g., missing app.slug) but updateEvent is still invoked, leading CalendarManager.updateEvent to no-op silently; should guard/continue when credential is undefined
handleCancelBooking.ts: DB credential refetch omits including app.slug and mapping to appName, so downstream code sees credential.appName undefined, breaking logs/emails/metrics keyed on appName
handleNewBooking.ts loadUsers dynamic-user branch no longer selects organization.slug, causing downstream user.organization?.slug to be undefined for dynamic bookings in org context
handleNewBooking.ts loadUsers host branch can return undefined instead of an array when eventType.users is undefined/null, causing later array operations (slice/push) to throw

Async callbacks passed to Array.forEach in tRPC reschedule path (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx:553) are not awaited, so calendar.deleteEvent runs after the mutation resolves and errors become unhandled rejections

Async callbacks passed to Array.forEach in vital reschedule (packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions are fire-and-forget and errors bypass the enclosing try/catch

Async callbacks passed to Array.forEach in wipemycalother reschedule (packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions are fire-and-forget and errors bypass the enclosing try/catch

Recurring-event deletion loop in handleCancelBooking (packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:460) uses forEach(async ...) so recurring deletions are not awaited by the cancel response/Promise.all and errors are orphaned

getCalendarCredentials in packages/core/CalendarManager.ts:28 assigns calendar = getCalendar(credential) without awaiting, so the returned calendar field is a Promise<Calendar|null> that can be mistakenly treated as Calendar|null by consumers

Dynamic import() calls stored directly in the app-store barrel object (packages/app-store/index.ts:1) execute eagerly at module initialization, defeating intended lazy loading and adding await overhead to lookups

getVideoAdapters in packages/core/videoClient.ts:21 now awaits appStore imports sequentially in a for...of loop, reducing parallelism and increasing latency compared to the prior parallel mapping approach
fetch_error_details pairs error_ids with events.values() from nodestore.get_multi, relying on dict value ordering that may not match node_ids, causing wrong error event data association
test_get_with_error only covers a single error event, so it cannot detect the potential mis-association bug in fetch_error_details
Environment filter (selection.environments) was removed from the /trace-items/attributes/ query, potentially broadening results across environments if the backend previously narrowed by environment

Detector validator accepts an owner actor from the request and writes owner_user_id/owner_team_id without explicitly validating the actor belongs to the project’s organization, risking cross-org IDOR-style assignment
schedule_webhook_delivery uses a 30s processing_deadline_duration which may be too short under load for the scheduling pass, leading to timeouts/pages; should be increased (e.g., 120s) and measured
Default project option sentry:autofix_automation_tuning changed from "low" to "off" without migration/release note/flag, potentially disabling autofix for projects that never set the option

feedback_summaries.make_input_prompt changed from bulleted list to '------' delimiter, altering LLM prompt structure and risking delimiter collisions when feedback contains '------'; should escape/choose safer delimiter and update evals/golden outputs accordingly

PR includes migrations 0917 and 0920 modified to early-return and reference 0921_convert_org_saved_searches_to_views_rerevised.py, but that migration file is not in the diff—risking a broken migration chain if it doesn’t already exist on main
